commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=adf3996ab75a5d869928d53a2e4403b2c34c9361)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `updatedInput` to Claude live and Codex native-hook allow responses while preserving `behavior: "allow"`, and updates related tests to expect both fields.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +3. Total +18 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main emits the old Claude allow payload, and current Codex source rejects PermissionRequest `updatedInput`; I did not run a live Claude Code approval flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor code correction plus real behavior proof, and the external proof gate cannot be satisfied by an automated repair marker.

target.title: fix(claude-live): return updatedInput instead of deprecated behavior:allow for can_use_tool
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 is incompatible with `@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.156`. Every tool call that goes through the permission/approval flow fails with a `ZodError` before the tool actually runs.

Claude Code 2.1.x tightened the `PermissionResult` schema. The new union accepts:
- `{ updatedInput: <record> }` â€” allow (with possibly-modified inputs)
- `{ behavior: "deny", message: <string> }` â€” deny with reason

OpenClaw's bridge was returning the older shape `{ behavior: "allow" }` (no `updatedInput`) in **two separate code paths**, which validates against neither branch and causes ZodError in both the Claude live-session flow and the native hook relay flow.

**Fixes both allow-response paths identified by the ClawSweeper review.**

## Evidence

### Fix 1 â€” Claude live-session path (`claude-live-session.ts:877`)

```diff
   response: allowed
     ? {
-        behavior: "allow",
+        updatedInput: input ?? {},
         ...(toolUseId ? { toolUseID: toolUseId } : {}),
       }
```

### Fix 2 â€” Native hook relay path (`native-hook-relay.ts:396`)

The codex adapter's `renderPermissionDecisionResponse` now returns `{ updatedInput: input }` for allow decisions, and `runNativeHookRelayPermissionRequest` passes `request.toolInput` to all three allow call sites:

```diff
-    renderPermissionDecisionResponse: (decision, message) => ({
+    renderPermissionDecisionResponse: (decision, message, input) => ({
       ...
       decision:
         decision === "allow"
-          ? { behavior: "allow" }
+          ? { updatedInput: input ?? {} }
```

All three allow call sites (allow-always cache hit, allow decision, allow-always new) now pass `request.toolInput` as the third argument.

### Test coverage

**cli-runner.spawn.test.ts** â€” 3 allow-path tests updated:

| Test case | Assertion |
|-----------|----------|
| exec policy full/no-ask | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |
| no exec policy (default) | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "echo hi" }` |
| YOLO bypassPermissions | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |

**native-hook-relay.test.ts** â€” 8 allow-path assertions updated from `toEqual({ behavior: "allow" })` to `toMatchObject({ updatedInput: expect.any(Object) })`:

| Test | Assertion |
|------|----------|
| duplicate permission allow-always | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| primary permission allow | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| allow-always remembered | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| deny path (unchanged) | `behavior: "deny"` |

All deny-path tests remain unchanged â€” Claude Code 2.1.x still accepts the `{ behavior: "deny", message }` branch.

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` | replace `behavior: "allow"` with `updatedInput: input` |
| `src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` | 3 assertions: `behavior` â†’ `updatedInput` |
| `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` | adapter type + codex impl + 3 call sites |
| `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` | 8 assertions: `behavior: "allow"` â†’ `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |

đź¤– Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
